name: Optimize LLGo compilation and CI speed

description: baseline ci subset after rebasing current branch onto goplus/main; cl package currently fails in local environment

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for representative Go test CI subset covering internal build/crosscompile/goembed and cl compiler tests after updating branch to latest goplus/main.
asi.error_details: go test ./cl failed after ~260s; output shows package foo dump and FAIL github.com/goplus/llgo/cl.
asi.rollback_reason: Baseline command failed; no code changes to evaluate.
asi.next_action_hint: Use a stable, faster benchmark subset first (build/test packages that pass locally), then separately investigate cl failure if needed.

name: Optimize LLGo stable core build/CI subset

description: baseline stable core CI subset after rebasing current branch onto latest goplus/main

asi.hypothesis: Use a stable locally passing subset of CI that exercises LLGo build orchestration, crosscompile helpers, and goembed tests as the primary optimization benchmark.

description: profiling internal/build test timings without code changes

asi.hypothesis: Measure per-test elapsed times to identify slow core CI cases before optimizing.
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement; profiling-only rerun was slower than baseline due to noise/JSON overhead.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect slow tests for redundant llgo builds/runs and opportunities to reuse setup or avoid unnecessary rebuilds.

description: profiled isolated TestTest to see compile hotspots

asi.hypothesis: CPU profile of the slowest internal/build test can reveal product compile hot spots relevant to CI speed.
asi.profile_summary: Isolated TestTest passed in 11.8s including profile overhead; sampled CPU mostly in clang/LLVM and llgo cl compileInstr paths, suggesting wall time includes external toolchain work and repeated integration builds.
asi.rollback_reason: Profiling-only run is not comparable to core benchmark and does not improve baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Prefer reducing redundant integration test builds or improving build cache reuse; CPU micro-optimizations are less obvious from this profile.

description: parallelize independent ldflags rewrite integration tests

asi.hypothesis: The ldflags rewrite integration tests are independent of the mockable/os.Stdout global tests, so allowing them to run in parallel can overlap repeated llgo builds and reduce package CI wall time without reducing coverage.
asi.result: Core subset improved from 29.5s to 27.5s; internal/build reported 26.426s. Other packages were served from go test cache in this run.

description: profiled current internal/build test timings after ldflags parallelization

asi.hypothesis: Re-measure per-test wall times after parallelizing ldflags tests to find remaining bottlenecks.
asi.rollback_reason: Profiling-only command is not the full core benchmark and uses -count=1/-json, so it should not be kept as an optimization result.
asi.next_action_hint: Explore whether TestRun, TestTest, TestExtest, TestCmpTest can safely share build artifacts or whether mockable can become test-local to permit more parallelism.

description: parallelize module hook mode-gen integration test

asi.hypothesis: The module hook ModeGen test is independent and can overlap with slower integration builds, reducing internal/build wall time.
asi.result: Full core subset passed but wall time was 28.6s, worse than current kept 27.5s; likely added contention or only noise-level benefit.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid adding more parallelism to small LLVM/compile tests unless reruns show benefit; focus on eliminating redundant builds instead.

description: parallelize independent ModeRun smoke test

asi.hypothesis: TestRun does not capture stdout or set process environment, and its mockRun path only needs mocked os.Exit for failures; in the passing path it can overlap with other integration builds to reduce internal/build wall time.
asi.result: Core subset passed and improved from kept 27.5s to 26.9s; internal/build reported 25.890s.

description: attempted to parallelize ModeCmpTest smoke test

asi.hypothesis: TestCmpTest might be independent enough to overlap with other integration builds like TestRun.
asi.error_details: Failed: concurrent mockRun tests race on global mockable.Exit. TestRun observed real os.Exit(0) after another parallel mockRun restored the global exit function.
asi.rollback_reason: Parallelizing mockRun users is unsafe without redesigning mockable or serializing, which would remove the speed benefit.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not parallelize additional mockRun tests. If pursuing later, make exit mocking context-local/injected rather than package-global.

description: confirmation rerun exposed flakiness from parallel LLVM build tests

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the current kept parallel test changes are stable under a repeated full core benchmark.
asi.error_details: go test crashed with SIGTRAP during cgo LLVMParseIRInContext from plan9asm while parallel ldflags rewrite tests were running; suggests concurrent LLVM context/use is unsafe or too flaky.
asi.rollback_reason: The kept test parallelism is not robust despite earlier speedup.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert parallelization of LLVM-heavy ldflags tests; keep only safe changes after repeated confirmation.

description: revert unsafe parallel LLVM ldflags tests while retaining ModeRun parallelism

asi.hypothesis: The SIGTRAP came from running multiple LLVM/plan9asm-heavy ldflags builds in parallel; reverting those t.Parallel calls should restore stability while retaining the less risky ModeRun parallelism.
asi.result: Core subset passed in 29.3s, slightly better than the original 29.5s baseline but slower than the unsafe/flaky parallel-ldflags best.

description: attempted confirmation rerun but go test cache made result non-comparable

asi.hypothesis: Confirm stability of the corrected test parallelism after reverting LLVM-heavy parallel tests.
asi.result: Command passed but all packages were cached, producing a non-comparable 0.5s run.
asi.rollback_reason: Go test cache invalidated the timing comparison; no code change to keep/discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Use -count=1 for confirmation/profiling runs or accept the prior uncached pass as the stability signal.

name: Optimize LLGo uncached core CI test subset

description: baseline uncached core CI subset after stability correction

asi.hypothesis: Switch the benchmark to use go test -count=1 so the primary metric measures uncached test/compile work and is not invalidated by Go's test cache.

description: narrow external-test smoke case to one representative package

asi.hypothesis: TestExtest only needs to verify that an external _test package can be built/run and reports PASS; using one representative package retains that coverage while avoiding repeated compilation of four nearly identical packages in the unit test suite.
asi.result: Uncached core subset improved from 28.1s to 26.9s; internal/build dropped from 27.505s to 26.184s. Secondary package times varied due parallel scheduling.

description: confirmation rerun of narrowed external-test smoke case showed noisy marginal gain

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the external-test narrowing remains faster under an uncached full core subset rerun.
asi.result: Run passed at 28.0s, still slightly below the 28.1s uncached baseline but much slower than the 26.9s first run; improvement is noise-level in full subset though internal/build coverage remains reduced.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation did not beat the current best primary metric; no new code changes beyond already kept commit.
asi.next_action_hint: Measure internal/build alone for this change or seek larger deterministic reductions; avoid relying on small noisy full-subset deltas.

description: profiled internal/build timings after narrowing external-test smoke

asi.hypothesis: Measure remaining slow internal/build tests after narrowing TestExtest to one external-test package.
asi.rollback_reason: Profiling-only command is not the full uncached core benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Largest remaining deterministic opportunities are TestTest and repeated ldflags rewrite builds; avoid unsafe LLVM parallelism.

description: narrow ModeTest smoke case to one representative internal test package

asi.hypothesis: TestTest is a ModeTest smoke test; using a single representative package with in-package tests still exercises llgo test build/run mechanics while avoiding extra dependency/main package compilation already covered by other tests such as ModeCmpTest and TestExtest.
asi.result: Uncached core subset improved from 28.1s baseline / 26.9s current best to 25.8s; internal/build dropped to 25.074s.

description: confirmation rerun for narrowed ModeTest smoke case

asi.hypothesis: Confirm that narrowing the ModeTest smoke case gives a stable uncached core CI improvement.
asi.result: Run passed in 26.0s, close to the kept 25.8s and well below the 28.1s baseline, supporting a real improvement despite not beating the current best.
asi.rollback_reason: No new code change; confirmation run was slightly slower than current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to find further reductions in ldflags rewrite tests or build cache setup.

description: remove fmt dependency from ldflags rewrite test fixture

asi.hypothesis: The ldflags rewrite fixture only needs deterministic string output and runtime version/GOROOT rewrites; using fmt.Printf pulls in a much larger dependency graph for each repeated binary build. Replacing fmt with builtin println keeps coverage while reducing compile/link work.
asi.result: Uncached core subset improved from 25.8s current best / 28.1s baseline to 21.4s; internal/build dropped to 20.866s.

description: confirmation rerun for fmt-free ldflags rewrite fixture

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the fmt-free ldflags rewrite fixture gives a stable uncached core CI speedup.
asi.result: Repeat run passed and improved slightly further to 21.1s; confirms the large reduction is real.

description: profiled internal/build timings after fmt-free ldflags fixture

asi.hypothesis: Measure remaining slow tests after removing the fmt dependency from the rewrite fixture.
asi.slow_tests: TestTest 8.23s, TestExtest 2.51s, TestLdFlagsRewriteVars 2.25s, TestBuildDo_DepFingerprintAndVersion 1.40s, TestRunPrintfWithStdioNobuf 1.15s, TestLdFlagsRewriteVarsMainAlias 1.07s.
asi.rollback_reason: Profiling-only command is not the full uncached core benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: The rewrite fixture optimization succeeded; remaining bottleneck is llgo test itself pulling in testing/runtime, less amenable to fixture trimming.

description: make in-package ModeTest smoke compile-only

asi.hypothesis: The in-package ModeTest smoke test primarily needs to exercise test binary construction; TestExtest still executes a ModeTest binary, so making TestTest compile-only avoids a redundant test process run while keeping build coverage.
asi.result: Uncached core subset improved from 21.1s current best to 20.7s; internal/build dropped to 19.912s.

description: switch ModeCmpTest smoke to simpler print fixture

asi.hypothesis: ModeCmpTest only needs to compare Go and LLGo output, so using the smaller print fixture might reduce compile work versus the runtest fixture.
asi.result: Run passed but primary metric was unchanged at 20.7s and internal/build was slightly slower than current best; coverage would also be narrower.
asi.rollback_reason: No measurable speed improvement and potential loss of coverage for a main package with imports.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep ModeCmpTest on runtest; focus elsewhere.

description: remove ModeRun t.Parallel to test whether it is still useful

asi.hypothesis: After reverting unsafe ldflags parallelism, TestRun may no longer overlap useful work; removing t.Parallel could simplify tests without hurting speed.
asi.result: Run passed but slowed to 21.6s, so TestRun parallelism still helps by overlapping with other package tests or tail work.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus current best 20.7s.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep TestRun t.Parallel; it appears safe when no other mockRun tests are parallel.

description: swap ModeTest execution coverage from external test to in-package test

asi.hypothesis: Running the smaller in-package test and compiling the external-test package without executing it might preserve ModeTest run coverage while reducing external test overhead.
asi.result: Run passed but slowed to 22.7s; in-package execution is more expensive than the current compile-only in-package plus external execution mix.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed and coverage tradeoff is not better.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep TestTest compile-only and TestExtest executing the representative external package.

description: convert redundant simple run smoke to build-only print smoke

asi.hypothesis: The simple print TestRun duplicates ModeRun execution coverage already exercised by TestRunPrintfWithStdioNobuf; converting it to a build-only print smoke (and renaming accordingly) preserves basic build coverage while avoiding one redundant process run.
asi.result: Uncached core subset improved from 20.7s current best to 19.0s; internal/build dropped to 18.294s.

description: confirmation rerun for build-only print smoke

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the build-only print smoke change is stable and not a one-off timing artifact.
asi.result: Repeat uncached core subset passed in 18.9s, confirming the speedup.

description: profiled internal/build timings after build-only print smoke

asi.hypothesis: Identify remaining slow tests after converting the redundant ModeRun smoke to build-only.
asi.slow_tests: TestTest 7.65s, TestExtest 2.58s, TestLdFlagsRewriteVars 2.54s, TestBuildDo_DepFingerprintAndVersion 1.50s, TestLdFlagsRewriteVarsMainAlias 1.27s, TestRunPrintfWithStdioNobuf 1.18s.
asi.rollback_reason: Profiling-only command is not the full uncached core benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Most remaining time is intrinsic llgo test compilation; only small fixture/test-scope reductions remain.

description: consolidate in-package and external ModeTest coverage into one fixture

asi.hypothesis: A single llgo test invocation can cover both in-package tests and external package tests if the external-test fixture also includes an internal test file. This preserves ModeTest coverage while eliminating a separate costly test binary build.
asi.result: Uncached core subset improved from 18.9s current best to 17.1s; internal/build dropped to 16.406s.

description: confirmation rerun for consolidated ModeTest fixture

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the consolidated internal/external ModeTest fixture gives a stable speedup.
asi.result: Repeat uncached core subset passed in 16.9s, confirming the improvement.

description: profiled internal/build after consolidating ModeTest fixture

asi.hypothesis: Measure remaining bottlenecks after eliminating the separate in-package ModeTest run.
asi.slow_tests: TestExtest 8.19s now includes both internal and external test coverage, TestLdFlagsRewriteVars 1.90s, TestBuildDo_DepFingerprintAndVersion 1.35s, TestRunPrintfWithStdioNobuf 1.11s, TestLdFlagsRewriteVarsMainAlias 1.06s.
asi.rollback_reason: Profiling-only command is not the full uncached core benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Further ModeTest reductions would require optimizing test binary compilation or reducing the fixture dependency graph without losing internal/external coverage.

description: make external ModeTest fixture not import package under test

asi.hypothesis: An external-test package that merely compiles may cover package-foo_test wiring while relying on the internal test for Foo behavior, possibly reducing dependency work.
asi.result: Run passed but slowed to 20.6s and narrowed external-test behavioral coverage.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed and coverage became weaker.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the external test importing/calling Foo; slowdown may be noise but coverage loss is not worthwhile.

description: removed runtime rewrite checks from ldflags fixture (coverage regression)

asi.hypothesis: Removing the runtime import and runtime.defaultGOROOT/runtime.buildVersion checks from the ldflags rewrite fixture would reduce compile cost.
asi.result: Speed improved to 15.7s, but this drops coverage for runtime package global string rewrites, which is not acceptable per the requirement to improve efficiency without reducing coverage.
asi.rollback_reason: Coverage regression: no longer tests runtime.defaultGOROOT/runtime.buildVersion rewriting.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep runtime rewrite coverage; seek efficiency by consolidating redundant tests or shrinking dependencies without removing asserted behavior.

description: parallelize goembed unit tests

asi.hypothesis: internal/goembed tests use isolated temp directories and no shared mutable state, so t.Parallel can preserve coverage while reducing package test wall time.
asi.result: Full uncached core subset passed, but primary metric regressed from current best 16.9s to 17.4s and goembed package timing was noisy/slower under concurrent load.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary metric improvement in the full CI subset despite preserving coverage.
asi.next_action_hint: If optimizing goembed specifically, benchmark package alone; for core CI, package-level parallelism is already enough and adding intra-package parallelism adds contention.

description: restore full ModeTest coverage while retaining coverage-preserving fixture optimizations

asi.hypothesis: Per the requirement not to reduce test coverage, restore the original ModeTest package scopes (runtest and runextest/...) while keeping coverage-preserving optimizations: fmt-free rewrite fixture and redundant simple run smoke converted to build-only because ModeRun is still covered by TestRunPrintfWithStdioNobuf.
asi.result: Uncached core subset is 24.4s versus original 28.1s baseline, preserving the original ModeTest coverage scopes while retaining a significant speedup.

description: cache detected LLVM version across build contexts

asi.hypothesis: Each build context previously spawned clang --version independently while collecting fingerprints. Caching the detected LLVM version by compiler path across contexts preserves coverage and correctness while reducing repeated process startup in tests and CI builds.
asi.result: Uncached core subset improved from coverage-preserving 24.4s to 21.8s; internal/build dropped to 21.034s.

description: refine LLVM version cache key to resolved compiler path

asi.hypothesis: Keying the LLVM-version cache by resolved compiler path and not storing empty failures is safer if PATH or compiler availability changes within a long process.
asi.result: Run passed but primary metric was unchanged at 21.8s compared with current best; this is a correctness refinement rather than a speed win.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary metric improvement under experiment rules, though the idea may be worth applying manually if cache safety concerns arise.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the simpler cached version for now or revisit if review requests PATH-sensitive cache behavior.

description: profiled internal/build after coverage-preserving speedups and LLVM version cache

asi.hypothesis: Measure remaining internal/build bottlenecks after restoring full ModeTest coverage and caching LLVM version detection.
asi.slow_tests: TestTest 8.20s, TestExtest 4.66s, TestLdFlagsRewriteVars 2.01s, TestBuildDo_DepFingerprintAndVersion 1.37s, TestRunPrintfWithStdioNobuf 1.11s, TestLdFlagsRewriteVarsMainAlias 1.05s.
asi.rollback_reason: Profiling-only command is not the full uncached core benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Coverage-preserving next steps likely require product-level build/test speedups rather than narrowing test scopes.

description: add explicit cross-context LLVM version cache test

asi.hypothesis: Add a focused unit test proving LLVM version detection is cached across build contexts so the product optimization has direct coverage without reducing existing coverage.
asi.result: Run passed but primary metric regressed from 21.8s to 24.3s due noise and the additional process-spawning unit test overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary metric improvement under the experiment loop; adding this test may still be review-desirable but hurts the speed target.
asi.next_action_hint: If reviewers require coverage for the cache, add a lighter injectable detector instead of spawning a shell helper.

description: confirmation rerun for coverage-preserving current state

asi.hypothesis: Confirm the current coverage-preserving state (full ModeTest scopes plus fmt-free rewrite fixture, build-only redundant smoke, and LLVM version cache) remains stable.
asi.result: Run passed in 21.5s, close to the kept 21.8s and substantially below the original 28.1s baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: No new code change; confirmation was slightly faster but does not require a separate code commit.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue looking for product-level optimizations that preserve test scope.

description: remove go work sync subprocess from dependency fingerprint test

asi.hypothesis: The dependency fingerprint test can likely rely on the handwritten go.work and module files without running go work sync, preserving coverage while avoiding an external go subprocess.
asi.result: Run passed, but primary metric regressed from current best 21.8s to 23.8s; any subprocess saving is lost in noise or not on the critical path.
asi.rollback_reason: No speed improvement under the benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Leave go work sync in place for clarity/stability unless optimizing this test in isolation.

name: Optimize Go CI build/test subset

description: baseline Go CI subset including explicit go build before tests

asi.hypothesis: Measure a CI-like sequence with the explicit go build step followed by the stable test subset so we can evaluate whether the build step is redundant with go test.

description: drop redundant explicit go build before go test in Go CI/local CI

asi.hypothesis: go test ./... already compiles all packages (including packages without tests), so the separate Go CI/local CI go build ./... step is redundant for coverage and can be removed to reduce CI wall time.
asi.result: CI-like subset improved from 25.4s to 24.6s while preserving test execution coverage; the measured gain is modest here because build artifacts were warm, but CI should avoid an entire redundant build step.

description: confirmation rerun for removing redundant go build step

asi.hypothesis: Confirm removing the explicit go build step remains faster on the CI-like subset.
asi.result: Repeat run passed and improved to 23.7s versus 25.4s baseline.

description: avoid installing torch in Go workflow where only numpy is needed

asi.hypothesis: The Go workflow runs Go tests, not demos; repository references show torch is only used by the _demo/py/tensor demo, while Go test coverage needs numpy/python embedding. Removing torch from this workflow preserves coverage and avoids a very large CI dependency installation.
asi.result: Stable Go subset still passed; primary subset timing also improved to 22.4s, though the main expected win is reduced CI setup time not captured by the Go-test-only benchmark.

name: Optimize llgo CI install step

description: baseline installing all repository commands/packages as in llgo workflow

asi.hypothesis: Measure the current llgo workflow install step, which runs go install ./... even though jobs only need the llgo binary.

description: install only llgo command in llgo workflow jobs

asi.hypothesis: llgo workflow jobs only invoke the llgo command after installation; installing every repository command with go install ./... is redundant. Installing ./cmd/llgo preserves coverage while reducing install work.
asi.result: Install benchmark improved from 1.7s to 1.3s locally; CI benefit should scale with cold caches and repeated jobs.

description: confirmation rerun for installing only llgo command

asi.hypothesis: Confirm go install ./cmd/llgo timing is stable for llgo workflow install optimization.
asi.result: Repeat install completed in 1.3s, matching kept result.
asi.rollback_reason: No new code change and metric ties current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep workflow change; continue looking for other repeated CI setup/install work.

description: install only llgo command in targets workflow too

asi.hypothesis: The targets workflow also only needs the llgo command to run embedded target builds, so replacing go install ./... with go install ./cmd/llgo preserves coverage and saves install work.
asi.result: Install command passed at 1.3s, but primary metric ties current best because the same command was already measured.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary improvement relative to current best under experiment rules, though the workflow edit is logically the same optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Apply this change together with the llgo workflow install narrowing during final cleanup if desired.

name: Optimize aggregate workflow llgo install commands

description: baseline aggregate install work across llgo and targets workflows

asi.hypothesis: Measure aggregate install time for the current workflow state: four llgo.yml jobs install ./cmd/llgo, while targets.yml still installs ./... .

description: install only llgo command in targets workflow aggregate

asi.hypothesis: The targets workflow only invokes llgo to build embedded target demos, so using go install ./cmd/llgo preserves coverage while reducing aggregate workflow install work.
asi.result: Aggregate install benchmark improved from 7.5s to 7.3s locally; cold CI should benefit more from avoiding extra command/package install work.

description: remove demo-only Python dependency install from llgo test shard job

asi.hypothesis: The llgo test shard job runs packages under ./test/... and repository search shows no numpy/torch usage there, so the demo-only Python dependency install can be removed without reducing test coverage.
asi.result: Install benchmark is unchanged at 7.5s because it does not measure pip setup; the workflow edit is logically beneficial but outside this metric.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary metric improvement in the active aggregate install benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Consider re-applying with a setup-focused benchmark or during final cleanup; expected CI win is avoiding repeated pip installs in llgo test shards.

name: Optimize hello workflow redundant matrix jobs

description: baseline hello workflow install work with two Go matrix versions

asi.hypothesis: Measure install work for the hello workflow's current 2 OS x 2 Go matrix. The test step only runs for Go 1.24, so Go 1.21 matrix entries appear to do setup/install without executing coverage.

description: remove no-op Go 1.21 hello matrix entries

asi.hypothesis: The hello workflow's Go 1.21 matrix entries do not run the hello test because the only test step is gated to Go 1.24; removing those no-op jobs should preserve actual coverage and reduce CI work.
asi.result: Local install simulation was slower due noise (8.1s for two installs), so the active metric did not improve despite halving workflow jobs.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary metric improvement under this noisy local install benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: This remains a strong CI-structure cleanup candidate: re-apply with a static job-count/setup-time benchmark or during final review if accepted.

name: Optimize hello workflow active job count

description: baseline hello workflow active matrix jobs

asi.hypothesis: Use active matrix job count as a direct CI resource metric for the hello workflow. Current matrix launches 2 OS x 2 Go versions, but only Go 1.24 executes the hello test step.

description: remove no-op Go 1.21 hello workflow jobs

asi.hypothesis: The Go 1.21 hello matrix entries perform checkout/setup/install but skip the only hello test step due to the Go 1.24 condition. Removing them halves active hello jobs without reducing actual executed test coverage.
asi.result: hello_active_jobs dropped from 4 to 2; the Go 1.24 hello test still runs on both Linux and macOS.

name: Optimize llgo test shard redundant setup-go steps

description: baseline setup-go steps in llgo test shard job

asi.hypothesis: The llgo test shard job has a single Go version in its matrix but still runs setup-go twice (build and testing), so step count can capture redundant setup work.

description: remove redundant setup-go step from llgo test shard job

asi.hypothesis: Because the llgo test shard matrix only uses Go 1.24.2, the build setup-go step already provides the same Go version for testing. Removing the second setup-go step preserves coverage and saves repeated CI setup/cache work across all shards.
asi.result: test_job_setup_go_steps dropped from 2 to 1 in the shard job.

name: Optimize llgo test shard Python dependency setup

description: baseline Python dependency count in llgo test shard job

asi.hypothesis: The llgo test shard job installs numpy and torch even though ./test/... has no numpy/torch/python imports; dependency count is a proxy for redundant CI setup work.

description: remove demo-only Python dependency install from llgo test shard job

asi.hypothesis: The llgo test shard job only runs llgo test over ./test/...; repository search shows no numpy/torch/python imports there, so removing the demo-only pip install preserves actual test coverage while eliminating redundant setup in every shard.
asi.result: test_job_python_deps dropped from 2 to 0.

name: Optimize llgo test shard unused matrix dimensions

description: baseline unused go matrix dimension in llgo test shard job

asi.hypothesis: After removing the redundant setup-go testing step, the llgo test shard job's go matrix dimension is unused and only adds workflow complexity.

description: remove unused go matrix dimension from llgo test shard job

asi.hypothesis: The llgo test shard job now uses a fixed Go 1.24.2 setup and no longer references matrix.go, so removing the unused matrix dimension preserves job count and coverage while simplifying workflow expansion.
asi.result: unused_test_matrix_dims dropped from 1 to 0; test_jobs remains 2 OS x 4 shards = 8.

name: Optimize hello workflow redundant setup-go step

description: baseline setup-go steps in hello workflow

asi.hypothesis: After removing the no-op Go 1.21 hello matrix entries, the hello workflow has only Go 1.24.2 but still runs setup-go twice.

description: remove redundant setup-go testing step from hello workflow

asi.hypothesis: The hello workflow now only tests Go 1.24.2, so the build setup-go step already provides the same Go version for the hello test. Removing the second setup-go preserves coverage and saves setup time.
asi.result: hello_setup_go_steps dropped from 2 to 1.

name: Validate current coverage-preserving core subset

description: validation baseline for current coverage-preserving core subset

asi.hypothesis: Re-establish a validation baseline for the stable core test subset after workflow-focused CI changes, ensuring current code changes still pass without reducing ModeTest scopes.

description: cache pkg-config cgo flag lookups within process

asi.hypothesis: Repeated cgo pkg-config directives for the same package and environment spawn pkg-config twice each time. Caching parsed cflags/ldflags by package name and pkg-config environment preserves coverage/correctness while reducing repeated subprocess work in builds and tests.
asi.result: Stable core validation subset improved from 26.2s to 24.5s; expected to help cgo-heavy CI/demo builds.

description: confirmation rerun for pkg-config cgo cache

asi.hypothesis: Confirm pkg-config cgo flag cache remains stable and beneficial on repeated core validation run.
asi.result: Repeat run passed and improved to 22.7s versus 26.2s validation baseline.

description: move cgo directive comment back above parseCgoDecl

asi.hypothesis: Keep the pkg-config cache code readable by moving the cgo directive format comment back to parseCgoDecl.
asi.result: Run passed, but metric regressed relative to current best due noise; this is a formatting/readability-only change.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary improvement under experiment rules, though this cleanup may be desirable in final review.
asi.next_action_hint: If the comment remains misplaced in final diff, re-apply readability fix during cleanup.

name: Optimize hello workflow unused matrix dimension

description: baseline unused hello go matrix dimension

asi.hypothesis: After narrowing hello to Go 1.24 only and removing the second setup-go, the go matrix dimension is no longer needed; it can be simplified to a fixed version input without changing job count or coverage.

description: remove unused go matrix dimension from hello workflow

asi.hypothesis: The hello workflow now has a single Go version, so using a fixed go-version input removes an unused matrix dimension without changing the two OS jobs or reducing coverage. Also kept the cgo pkg-config cache comment attached to parseCgoDecl for readability.
asi.result: unused_hello_matrix_dims dropped from 1 to 0; hello_jobs remains 2.

name: Final current core validation after CI workflow cleanups

description: final validation baseline for current core subset

asi.hypothesis: Validate the current accumulated coverage-preserving product/test changes after workflow cleanup experiments by rerunning the stable core test subset.

name: Improve LLVM version cache failure behavior

description: attempted baseline for LLVM empty-cache store check but detector was flawed

asi.hypothesis: Check whether getLLVMVersion stores empty detection results in the global cache; intended as a correctness/safety cleanup metric.
asi.result: The static detector returned 0 because it matched the early non-empty context cache guard, so this baseline is not useful.
asi.next_action_hint: If pursuing, use a more precise detector or simply inspect/edit getLLVMVersion manually.

name: Avoid caching empty LLVM version detections

description: baseline empty LLVM version global cache store site

asi.hypothesis: getLLVMVersion currently stores the detected version in the global cache even when detection returns an empty string, which can make a transient clang lookup failure sticky for the process.

description: avoid globally caching empty LLVM version detection results

asi.hypothesis: Only cache non-empty LLVM version detections globally so transient clang lookup failures are not sticky; contexts still return the empty value for the current call, preserving behavior while improving robustness.
asi.result: empty_llvm_global_store_sites dropped from 1 to 0.

name: Post-cache-safety core validation

description: validation baseline after LLVM cache safety tweak

asi.hypothesis: Validate stable core subset after changing LLVM version cache to avoid storing empty detections.
asi.result: Core subset passed.

name: Improve pkg-config cache environment key coverage

description: baseline pkg-config cache only tracks selected environment variables

asi.hypothesis: The pkg-config cache key currently tracks selected environment variables; untracked PKG_CONFIG_* variables such as sysroot settings could make cached flags stale if they change within a process.

description: include all PKG_CONFIG environment variables in cache key

asi.hypothesis: Keying cached pkg-config results by all PKG_CONFIG* environment variables plus PATH prevents stale cache hits when sysroot, libdir, path, or other pkg-config controls change within a process, preserving correctness of the speed optimization.
asi.result: pkg_config_untracked_env_classes dropped from 1 to 0.

name: Validate pkg-config cache env-key final core

description: validation baseline after pkg-config env-key cache safety

asi.hypothesis: Validate stable core subset after broadening the pkg-config cache key to include all PKG_CONFIG* environment variables.
asi.result: Core subset passed.

name: Optimize targets workflow step count

description: baseline separate targets workflow build steps

asi.hypothesis: The targets workflow builds two target variants in separate GitHub Actions steps, which adds step overhead while not increasing coverage beyond running both commands.

description: combine targets workflow build variants into one step

asi.hypothesis: Running both target build variants in one step preserves coverage (both build.sh empty and build.sh defer still execute) while reducing GitHub Actions step overhead.
asi.result: targets_build_steps dropped from 2 to 1.

asi.result: Stable core subset passed.

description: repeat stable core subset after pruning stale ideas

asi.hypothesis: Confirm current stable core timing after pruning stale ideas from the backlog; no code change intended beyond autoresearch metadata.
asi.result: Run passed but was slower than stable_core_seconds baseline 24.1s, likely noise in internal/build integration tests.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary improvement; metadata-only pruning should not be committed as an optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue exploring coverage-preserving optimizations; avoid relying on small timing deltas.

description: profile current internal/build test timings

asi.hypothesis: Measure current internal/build per-test timings to identify remaining bottlenecks while preserving full test coverage.
asi.slow_tests: TestTest 8.31s, TestExtest 5.17s, TestLdFlagsRewriteVars 2.41s, TestBuildDo_DepFingerprintAndVersion 1.39s, TestRunPrintfWithStdioNobuf 1.14s, TestLdFlagsRewriteVarsMainAlias 1.21s.
asi.rollback_reason: Profiling-only command is not the full stable core benchmark.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining bottlenecks are ModeTest integration tests; reducing them without losing coverage likely requires product-level test build speedups or safe result caching rather than narrowing fixture scope.

name: Cover LLVM version cache without subprocess helper

description: baseline for LLVM cache test subprocess count

asi.hypothesis: Establish a baseline before considering adding coverage for the LLVM version global cache without introducing subprocess-heavy tests. No such test currently exists, so subprocess count is 0.

name: Fix PR CI cl select interleaving failure

description: baseline reproduces PR CI select interleaving failure

asi.hypothesis: Reproduce the failing PR CI test locally. The select fixture was updated during rebase to output deterministic send and receive checks, but the allowed-output list in cl/compile_test.go was not updated.

description: accept updated deterministic select fixture output in cl test

asi.hypothesis: Adding the updated select fixture output to the allowed-output set fixes the PR CI failure without reducing coverage; it reflects the current test fixture behavior after rebasing/updating cl/_testgo/select.
asi.result: Focused cl select test now passes.

name: Add lightweight LLVM version cache coverage

description: baseline no explicit cross-context LLVM cache test

asi.hypothesis: The LLVM version global cache optimization lacks a direct lightweight test. Baseline count of explicit cross-context cache tests is zero.

description: add lightweight cross-context LLVM version cache test

name: Remove unused singleton LLVM matrix dimensions

description: baseline singleton LLVM matrix dimensions in workflows

asi.hypothesis: Several workflows define a matrix dimension llvm: [19] with only one value. Since it does not multiply coverage, replacing matrix.llvm with a literal can reduce workflow matrix complexity without reducing executed jobs.

description: replace singleton LLVM matrix dimensions with literal LLVM version

asi.hypothesis: Replacing llvm: [19] singleton matrix dimensions with literal llvm-version: 19 preserves the same LLVM version and job coverage while simplifying workflow matrices and job expansion.
asi.result: singleton_llvm_matrix_dims dropped from 7 to 0 and workflow YAML parsed successfully.

name: Preserve CI check names while optimizing

description: baseline removed singleton LLVM matrix dimensions that may alter check names

asi.hypothesis: Although removing singleton llvm matrix dimensions simplifies workflows, it can change GitHub check names on an active PR and risk branch protection/status expectations. Count removed dimensions as a compatibility risk to reduce.

description: restore singleton LLVM matrices to preserve existing check names

asi.hypothesis: Restoring llvm: [19] singleton matrices preserves the existing GitHub Actions check names on the active PR while retaining all functional optimizations; this avoids risking branch-protection/status-name churn for negligible matrix simplification benefit.
asi.result: removed_llvm_matrix_dims dropped from 7 to 0 and workflow YAML parsed successfully.

name: Add lightweight pkg-config cache coverage

description: baseline no explicit pkg-config cache unit coverage

description: add lightweight pkg-config cache reuse unit test

asi.hypothesis: Injecting pkg-config command output lets us directly test that repeated #cgo pkg-config directives reuse cached cflags/ldflags without spawning subprocesses, increasing coverage of the cache optimization without weakening existing tests.
asi.result: Focused pkg-config cache test passed and explicit cache test count increased from 0 to 1.

name: Validate internal build after cache coverage tests

name: Enable parallel demo tests in CI

description: baseline llgo workflow host demo job parallelism

asi.hypothesis: The demo test script supports LLGO_DEMO_JOBS parallelism, but the LLGo workflow host demo step currently runs it sequentially. Increasing jobs can preserve coverage while reducing CI wall time.

description: run host demo tests with four parallel jobs in LLGo workflow

asi.hypothesis: Using LLGO_DEMO_JOBS=4 in the host demo step exercises the same demo set while overlapping independent llgo run invocations; the script already aggregates failures and logs, so coverage is preserved.
asi.result: Static workflow check confirms host demo parallelism increased from 1 to 4 and YAML parses.

name: Optimize optional torch dependency probe

description: baseline optional torch dependency probe using import torch

asi.hypothesis: test_demo.sh probes optional torch support by importing torch, which may be slow when torch is installed. Measure local probe cost before considering a faster availability check.

description: use importlib.find_spec for optional torch demo probe

asi.hypothesis: Checking importlib.util.find_spec('torch') avoids importing torch just to decide whether to skip the optional tensor demo, reducing demo setup overhead while still detecting installation presence.
asi.result: Local probe improved from 24.6ms to 16.7ms, but this weakens the probe: a broken torch installation would now run the demo and fail instead of being skipped as missing optional dependency.
asi.rollback_reason: Potential semantic/coverage change for broken optional dependency environments is not worth the small local speedup.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep import torch probe unless CI setup time shows torch import is a major bottleneck and broken-install behavior is acceptable.

name: Make demo parallel runner portable to macOS bash

description: baseline macOS demo parallelism falls back to sequential bash

asi.hypothesis: On macOS, /bin/bash is often 3.2 and lacks wait -n -p, so the demo script falls back to jobs=1 even when CI requests LLGO_DEMO_JOBS=4.

description: use portable FIFO wait loop for parallel demo runner

asi.hypothesis: Replacing wait -n -p with a portable FIFO wait loop lets LLGO_DEMO_JOBS work on older Bash versions such as macOS /bin/bash 3.2. It still runs the same demo cases and aggregates all failures, preserving coverage while enabling parallel host demo CI on macOS too.
asi.result: Static effective macOS demo jobs increased from 1 to 4 and shell syntax validates.

description: validate portable demo runner with fake llgo

asi.hypothesis: Run the demo script with a fake llgo binary to validate the portable parallel runner launches and aggregates all discovered host demo cases without relying on wait -n -p.
asi.result: Fake runner reported 84/84 tests passed with LLGO_DEMO_JOBS=4.
asi.rollback_reason: Validation-only run ties the current best metric; no new code change to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep portable FIFO wait loop; consider real demo CI feedback after next push.

name: Validate stable core after cache coverage and demo runner changes

description: validate stable core after cache coverage and demo runner changes

asi.hypothesis: Run the stable core subset after adding lightweight cache coverage tests and the portable demo runner to catch regressions before the next push.
asi.result: Stable core subset passed.

name: Preserve efficient wait-n demo scheduling on modern bash

description: baseline portable demo runner lacks wait-n fast path

asi.hypothesis: The portable FIFO demo scheduler enables macOS parallelism but no longer uses wait -n -p on modern Bash, which can leave slots idle on Linux. Add a hybrid fast path to preserve efficient scheduling where available.
